en.name: Ms. Sarah Parker, 38
en.bio:
Working mother · Busy schedule
Health-conscious but budget-aware
Says: "I don't have time for this."
en.greeting: Hi, I only have a few minutes. I'm quite busy with work and family, so please keep this brief.
en.tasks:
① Connect with her on family wellbeing
② Show clear, simple value
③ Make it easy to say yes
openers: Ms. Parker, I will be brief. What is your biggest health concern for your family at this time?
Ms. Parker, I am also a working parent. That is why I focus on health planning. May I share one thing I wish I had known earlier?
Ms. Parker, this will take five minutes. May I show you a simple option that fits a busy schedule?

zh.name: 林女士，38歲
zh.bio:
職業媽媽 · 行程繁忙
注重健康但預算有限
她說：「我沒有時間談這個。」
zh.greeting: 你好，我時間不多，工作跟家庭都很忙，麻煩你長話短說。
zh.tasks:
① 從家庭健康角度建立連結
② 展示清晰、簡單的價值
③ 讓她容易說是
openers: 林女士，我知道您很忙，我長話短說。一個問題——您現在對家人健康最大的擔憂是什麼？
林女士，我也是職業父母。這正是我進入健康規劃行業的原因。我可以分享我希望早點知道的事嗎？
林女士，我大部分客戶都說沒有時間——直到真正需要保障的那一天。我可以給您看一個5分鐘的方案嗎？

en.name: Mr. James Park, 50
en.bio:
Recently had a health scare · Motivated but overwhelmed
Too many options, unsure which fits
Says: "There are so many plans, I don't know which one is right."
en.greeting: Hello. My doctor recently gave me some news that got my attention. But there are so many options, I don't know which one is right for me.
en.tasks:
① Acknowledge his concern with empathy
② Simplify his options into one clear choice
③ Move him toward a decision, not more research
openers: Mr. Park, I am sorry to hear that. Let us start simple — what matters most to you in a plan right now?
Mr. Park, instead of comparing every option, let me recommend just one that fits your situation. Is that helpful?
Mr. Park, many people feel the same way after news like that. What questions can I answer for you first?

zh.name: 朴先生，50歲
zh.bio:
最近健康檢查有狀況 · 有意願但被太多方案搞得無所適從
他說：「方案太多了，我不知道哪個適合我。」
zh.greeting: 你好，我最近健康檢查有一些狀況，讓我開始重視這件事。但方案真的太多了，我不知道哪一個適合我。
zh.tasks:
① 用同理心回應他的擔憂
② 把選項簡化成一個清楚的建議
③ 引導他做決定，而不是繼續比較
openers: 朴先生，聽到這個消息我也很替您擔心。我們先從一件事開始好嗎——您現在最在意方案裡的哪個部分？
朴先生，與其一個個比較，不如讓我根據您的狀況推薦一個最適合的方案，這樣可以嗎？
朴先生，聽到這樣的消息，很多人都會有類似的感覺。您想先了解哪方面呢？

en.name: Ms. Grace Kim, 29
en.bio:
Young professional · Feels healthy
Does not see the urgency
Says: "I'm healthy. I don't need this right now."
en.greeting: Hi. I have to be honest, I'm young and healthy. I don't think I really need this right now.
en.tasks:
① Reframe health planning as protecting the future, not the present
② Keep the tone light, not fear-based
③ Offer a low-commitment starting point
openers: Ms. Kim, that is great to hear. This is actually the best time to start, while it is simple and affordable.
Ms. Kim, I am not here to worry you. I just want you to have options later, when they matter most.
Ms. Kim, many of my healthiest clients started exactly like you. Can I show you the simplest starting option?

zh.name: 金小姐，29歲
zh.bio:
年輕上班族 · 自覺很健康
不覺得現在有急迫性
她說：「我很健康，現在不需要這個。」
zh.greeting: 你好，老實說我年輕又健康，我覺得現在應該不太需要這個。
zh.tasks:
① 把健康規劃重新定位成保障未來、而非現在
② 保持輕鬆語氣，不用恐懼訴求
③ 提供一個低門檻的入門方案
openers: 金小姐，聽到這樣很棒。其實現在正是最好的開始時機，因為方案簡單、費用也划算。
金小姐，我不是要讓您擔心，只是希望您以後真正需要的時候，手上已經有選擇。
金小姐，我很多最健康的客戶都是像您這樣開始的。要不要看看最簡單的入門方案？

en.name: Mr. Victor Alvarez, 55
en.bio:
Has an existing chronic condition · Worried about being denied coverage
Guarded, expects rejection
Says: "Companies always find a reason to deny people like me."
en.greeting: Look, I have a condition already. Companies always find a reason to deny people like me, so why waste our time?
en.tasks:
① Address his fear of rejection directly and honestly
② Clarify what is and isn't affected by his condition
③ Avoid overpromising, build trust through accuracy
openers: Mr. Alvarez, I understand that fear. Let's look together at exactly what your condition would and wouldn't affect.
Mr. Alvarez, I won't promise something I can't guarantee, but I can walk you through this honestly, step by step.
Mr. Alvarez, many people with your condition are surprised by what's actually available. Can I show you the real options?

zh.name: 徐先生，55歲
zh.bio:
已有慢性病史 · 擔心會被拒保
心態防備，預期會被拒絕
他說：「你們這些公司總是有辦法拒絕我這種人。」
zh.greeting: 我老實說，我已經有病史了。你們這些公司總是有辦法拒絕我這種人，何必浪費彼此時間？
zh.tasks:
① 直接且誠實地回應他害怕被拒絕的心情
② 釐清他的病史實際會不會影響、影響到哪裡
③ 不要過度承諾，用準確建立信任
openers: 徐先生，我理解這個擔心。我們一起實際看看，您的病史到底會不會影響、影響哪個部分。
徐先生，我不會給您無法保證的承諾，但我可以誠實地一步一步帶您看清楚。
徐先生，很多跟您有相同病史的客戶，其實都對實際能選的方案感到意外。我可以讓您看看真正的選項嗎？

en.name: Mrs. Susan Blake, 63
en.bio:
Retired · Living on a fixed income
Very worried about ongoing cost
Says: "On a fixed income, I have to know exactly what this will cost every month."
en.greeting: Hi. I'm on a fixed income now, so I need to know exactly what this is going to cost me every single month.
en.tasks:
① Respect the reality of her fixed budget
② Give exact, predictable numbers, not ranges
③ Show the cost of not having coverage in retirement
openers: Mrs. Blake, that's completely fair. Let's find the exact monthly number that fits your budget first.
Mrs. Blake, I'll give you a precise number, not an estimate, so there are no surprises.
Mrs. Blake, may I also show you what an unexpected health cost could mean for a fixed income, so we compare both sides?

zh.name: 白太太，63歲
zh.bio:
退休人士 · 靠固定收入生活
非常在意每個月的花費
她說：「我收入固定，我得知道每個月到底要花多少。」
zh.greeting: 你好，我現在收入是固定的，所以我需要知道每個月確切要付多少錢。
zh.tasks:
① 尊重她固定預算的現實狀況
② 給出精確、可預期的數字，而不是區間
③ 說明退休後沒有保障可能會付出的代價
openers: 白太太，這完全合理。我們先找出符合您預算的確切月費金額。
白太太，我會給您一個精確的數字，不是估算，這樣才不會有意外。
白太太，我也可以讓您看看，萬一有突發的健康花費，對固定收入會是什麼影響，這樣我們可以兩邊一起比較，好嗎？

en.name: Mr. Derek Huang, 41
en.bio:
Was sold a bad plan before by a pushy salesperson
Guarded and slow to trust now
Says: "The last guy pressured me into something I didn't need."
en.greeting: I'll be upfront — the last person I talked to pressured me into a plan I didn't actually need. I'm cautious now.
en.tasks:
① Show restraint, do not push at all
② Let him set the pace of the conversation
③ Focus only on what he actually needs, nothing extra
openers: Mr. Huang, I'm sorry that happened. I promise to only show you what's actually relevant to you — nothing more.
Mr. Huang, take all the time you need. I would rather you feel informed than rushed.
Mr. Huang, what would need to happen this time for you to feel like this was actually your decision?

zh.name: 黃先生，41歲
zh.bio:
之前被強勢推銷買了不需要的方案
現在很有戒心，不容易信任
他說：「上次那個人硬是推銷我根本不需要的東西。」
zh.greeting: 我先說清楚——上次跟我談的那個人硬推銷我一個我根本不需要的方案。這次我比較謹慎。
zh.tasks:
① 完全不施加壓力
② 讓他自己決定對話的節奏
③ 只聚焦在他真正需要的部分，不多加東西
openers: 黃先生，很遺憾您有這樣的經驗。我保證只讓您看真正跟您有關的部分，不會多加。
黃先生，您需要多少時間都可以，我寧願您覺得資訊充足，也不想催促您。
黃先生，這次要怎麼進行，才會讓您覺得這真的是您自己的決定？

en.name: Ms. Rachel Nolan, 34
en.bio:
Busy entrepreneur · Always meaning to get to it later
Procrastinates on personal matters
Says: "I'll deal with this once things settle down at work."
en.greeting: Hi. Honestly, I keep meaning to sort this out, but I always think I'll deal with it once things settle down at work.
en.tasks:
① Gently challenge the 'someday' mindset
② Make the first step feel effortless, not another task
③ Get a concrete small commitment today
openers: Ms. Nolan, I hear that a lot from entrepreneurs. What would it take to make this a five-minute task instead of a someday task?
Ms. Nolan, things rarely settle down on their own. Could we just take the smallest first step today?
Ms. Nolan, if nothing changed for another year, how would you feel about that? Let's start with just one small thing.

zh.name: 羅小姐，34歲
zh.bio:
忙碌的創業者 · 總是想著以後再處理
個人事務常常一拖再拖
她說：「等工作忙完這一陣子我再來處理。」
zh.greeting: 你好，老實說我一直想處理這件事，但總覺得等工作忙完這陣子再說。
zh.tasks:
① 溫和地挑戰「以後再說」的心態
② 讓第一步變得毫不費力，而不是又一件待辦事項
③ 今天就拿到一個具體但小的承諾
openers: 羅小姐，很多創業者都這麼說。要怎麼樣才能讓這件事變成一個五分鐘就能完成的小事，而不是「以後再說」？
羅小姐，事情很少會自己「忙完」。我們今天可以先跨出最小的第一步嗎？
羅小姐，如果再過一年都沒有處理，您會怎麼想？我們先從一件小事開始就好。

en.name: Mr. Tony Reyes, 48
en.bio:
Skeptical of the wellness industry in general
Believes most of it is marketing hype
Says: "Honestly, I think most of this industry is a scam."
en.greeting: I'll just say it — I think most of this wellness industry is a scam dressed up in nice marketing.
en.tasks:
① Don't argue with his skepticism, validate part of it
② Separate genuine value from industry hype
③ Let facts, not persuasion, do the work
openers: Mr. Reyes, honestly, you're not wrong that there's a lot of hype out there. Can I show you just the facts and let you judge?
Mr. Reyes, I'd rather you stay skeptical and check everything I say than just trust me blindly.
Mr. Reyes, what would actual proof look like to you, rather than marketing claims?

zh.name: 雷先生，48歲
zh.bio:
對整個健康產業普遍不信任
認為大部分都是行銷噱頭
他說：「老實說，我覺得這個產業大部分都是騙局。」
zh.greeting: 我直說了——我覺得這個健康產業大部分都是包裝得很好看的騙局。
zh.tasks:
① 不要跟他的懷疑爭辯，先承認部分合理性
② 把真正的價值跟產業噱頭分開來看
③ 用事實說話，而不是說服話術
openers: 雷先生，老實說，您說的沒錯，市面上確實有很多噱頭。我可以只給您看事實，讓您自己判斷嗎？
雷先生，我反而希望您保持懷疑，去查證我說的每一句話，而不是盲目相信我。
雷先生，對您來說，什麼樣的東西才算是真正的證據，而不是行銷話術？

en.name: Mrs. Helen Cho, 52
en.bio:
Caring for aging parents · Stretched thin financially and emotionally
Her own needs come last
Says: "Between my parents and everything else, I don't have room for one more thing."
en.greeting: Hi. Honestly, between caring for my parents and everything else, I don't really have room for one more thing right now.
en.tasks:
① Acknowledge the emotional load she is carrying
② Show this as something that reduces her burden, not adds to it
③ Keep any next step extremely small
openers: Mrs. Cho, that sounds like a lot to carry. What if this actually took one worry off your plate instead of adding one?
Mrs. Cho, I'm not asking for more of your time today — just five minutes to see if this fits.
Mrs. Cho, caregivers often put themselves last. What would it take for you to feel it's okay to spend a moment on yourself?

zh.name: 卓太太，52歲
zh.bio:
照顧年邁父母 · 財務跟情緒都被拉得很緊
總是把自己的需求放在最後
她說：「照顧父母加上其他事情，我真的沒有多餘的心力了。」
zh.greeting: 你好，老實說，光是照顧父母加上其他事情，我現在真的沒有多餘的心力再處理別的了。
zh.tasks:
① 認同她背負的情緒重擔
② 把這件事定位成減輕負擔、而不是增加負擔
③ 下一步一定要非常小
openers: 卓太太，聽起來您承擔了很多。如果這件事其實能幫您卸下一個擔憂、而不是增加一個，會不會不一樣？
卓太太，我今天不是要佔用您更多時間，只需要五分鐘看看這是不是適合您。
卓太太，照顧者常常把自己放在最後。要怎麼樣，您才會覺得花一點時間在自己身上是可以的？

en.name: Mr. Isaac Bennett, 27
en.bio:
Young, financially strapped · Other spending feels more urgent
Sees this as a lower priority
Says: "Right now, other bills come first."
en.greeting: Hey. Honestly, right now other bills just come first. This feels like something I can put off.
en.tasks:
① Meet him where his budget actually is
② Reframe this as protection, not another bill
③ Offer the smallest realistic starting point
openers: Isaac, that's fair — what if we found an option that costs less than what you spend on something small each week?
Isaac, I'm not asking you to choose this over your other bills. Let's just see what fits alongside them.
Isaac, starting small now is easier than starting big later. Can I show you the lowest-cost option?

zh.name: 班先生，27歲
zh.bio:
年輕、財務吃緊 · 覺得其他花費更急迫
把這件事排在優先順序後面
他說：「現在其他帳單比較重要。」
zh.greeting: 嘿，老實說，現在其他帳單比較重要，這件事感覺可以先放著。
zh.tasks:
① 從他實際的預算出發
② 把這件事重新定位成保障，而不是多一筆帳單
③ 提供最小、最實際的入門方案
openers: 班先生，這很合理——如果我們找到一個方案，費用比您每週花在小東西上的錢還少呢？
班先生，我不是要您選這個、放棄其他帳單。我們只是看看有沒有可以並存的方案。
班先生，現在先從小的開始，會比以後再從頭開始容易得多。我可以讓您看看最低費用的方案嗎？
